004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.

006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *

012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.reactive;
018
019import static jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022import java.util.*;
023import java.util.concurrent.*;
024import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
025import java.util.function.*;
026import java.util.logging.*;
027
028import org.apache.juneau.http.response.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.marshall.marshaller.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.marshall.sse.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.util.*;
035
036import jakarta.servlet.*;
037import jakarta.servlet.http.*;
038
039/**
040 * Response processor that bridges <a class="doclink" href="https://www.reactive-streams.org/">Reactive Streams</a>
041 * return values from {@code @RestOp} handlers to Juneau's response pipeline.
042 *
043 * <h5 class='topic'>Activation (opt-in)</h5>
044 * <p>
045 * This processor ships in the opt-in {@code juneau-rest-server-reactive} module and is <b>not</b> wired
046 * into {@code DefaultConfig}. A bare {@code juneau-rest-server} has zero reactive behavior. When the
047 * {@code juneau-rest-server-reactive} jar is on the classpath, its
048 * {@code META-INF/services/org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor.ResponseProcessor} provider file is discovered
049 * by {@code RestContext} via {@link java.util.ServiceLoader} and this processor is front-loaded ahead of
050 * {@code AsyncResponseProcessor} in the chain &mdash; no {@code @Rest(responseProcessors=...)} entry required.
051 *
052 * <h5 class='topic'>What it handles</h5>
053 * <p>
054 * This is the single, shared spine for all reactive return-type support. It natively understands the
055 * JDK type {@link java.util.concurrent.Flow.Publisher Flow.Publisher&lt;T&gt;} (no external dependency
056 * required) and, through registered {@link ReactiveStreamsAdapter} providers, any third-party reactive
057 * type the opt-in {@code juneau-rest-server-reactive-reactor} module adapts to it (Project Reactor
058 * {@code Mono} / {@code Flux}, RxJava 3 {@code Single} / {@code Maybe} / {@code Flowable} /
059 * {@code Observable}, and the Reactive-Streams {@code org.reactivestreams.Publisher}).
060 *
061 * <p>
062 * Single-value reactive types (e.g. {@code Mono}, {@code Single}) are adapted to a {@link CompletionStage}
063 * and collapse onto the existing {@link AsyncResponseProcessor} async path, inheriting its timeout,
064 * completion-executor ({@code @Rest(asyncCompletionExecutor)}), and MDC bridging behavior for free.
065 *
066 * <h5 class='topic'>Response shapes for multi-value streams</h5>
067 * <p>
068 * A streaming publisher is rendered as one of three shapes, selected by the negotiated response media
069 * type (the handler's {@link RestResponse#setContentType(String) Content-Type}, then the request
070 * {@code Accept} header):
071 * <ul>
072 *    <li><b>SSE</b> ({@code text/event-stream}) &mdash; each element is emitted as a Server-Sent-Events
073 *       frame. {@link SseEvent} elements are written verbatim; any other element type is JSON-encoded
074 *       into the {@code data:} field.
075 *    <li><b>NDJSON</b> ({@code application/x-ndjson}, {@code application/jsonl}, {@code application/json5l}) &mdash; each element is
076 *       JSON-encoded on its own line.
077 *    <li><b>Buffer</b> (default, any other media type) &mdash; all elements are collected into a
078 *       {@link java.util.List List} and serialized through the normal serializer chain (e.g. a JSON
079 *       array). The collection is wrapped in a {@link CompletableFuture} and handed to the async path,
080 *       so a slow producer never blocks the request thread.
081 * </ul>
082 *
083 * <h5 class='topic'>Backpressure</h5>
084 * <p>
085 * Streaming subscribers request one element at a time ({@code request(1)} on subscribe and again after
086 * each frame is written and flushed). Because writing to the servlet output stream blocks until the
087 * socket accepts the bytes, this gives natural backpressure &mdash; the producer is paced by the
088 * client's drain rate and the server-side buffer does not grow without bound. Buffer-shape subscribers
089 * request {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} since the collection is bounded by the publisher's own completion.
090 *
091 * <h5 class='topic'>Threading, executors, and MDC</h5>
092 * <p>
093 * Buffer-shape responses route through {@link AsyncResponseProcessor} and therefore honor
094 * {@code @Rest(asyncCompletionExecutor)} and the SLF4J MDC bridge. Streaming-shape
095 * frame writes happen on whichever thread the publisher emits on (the Reactor / RxJava scheduler); this
096 * processor does not impose a {@code subscribeOn(...)} so it never fights the library's scheduler model.
097 * When MDC propagation is enabled, the request-thread MDC snapshot is reinstalled around each
098 * {@code onNext} / terminal callback via {@link MdcAsyncListener} so log statements emitted while writing
099 * a frame see the request's diagnostic context.
100 *
101 * <h5 class='topic'>Synchronous fallback</h5>
102 * <p>
103 * In environments where {@link HttpServletRequest#startAsync()} is unsupported (notably Juneau's
104 * {@code MockServletRequest}), streaming subscribes synchronously and blocks the request thread until
105 * the publisher terminates (bounded by the configured async timeout), writing frames as they arrive.
106 * This keeps the unit-test surface working without a real servlet container.
107 *
108 * @see ReactiveStreamsAdapter
109 * @see AsyncResponseProcessor
110 * @since 10.0.0
111 */
112@SuppressWarnings({
113   "resource" // Closeables here are framework-managed and not owned/closed by this class; not a real leak.
114})
115public class ReactiveResponseProcessor implements ResponseProcessor {
116
117   private static final Logger LOG = Logger.getLogger(ReactiveResponseProcessor.class.getName());
118
119   @SuppressWarnings({
120      "java:S3077" // Write-once double-checked-locking cache holding an immutable List.copyOf(...); volatile gives correct safe publication of the one-time assignment and the reference is never mutated afterward.
121   })
122   private static volatile List<ReactiveStreamsAdapter> adapters;
123
124   private enum Shape { BUFFER, SSE, NDJSON }
125
126   @FunctionalInterface
127   private interface FrameEncoder {
128      void write(FinishablePrintWriter w, Object element) throws IOException, SerializeException;
129   }
130
131   @Override /* Overridden from ResponseProcessor */
132   public int process(RestOpSession opSession) throws IOException, BasicHttpException {
133      var res = opSession.getResponse();
134      var content = res.getContent().orElse(null);
135
136      if (content == null) // HTT: true branch requires a response with no content at all; framework-level null-content suppression makes this unreachable in practice
137         return NEXT;
138
139      if (content instanceof Flow.Publisher<?> pub)
140         return dispatch(opSession, pub);
141
142      var a = adapters();
143      for (var adapter : a) {
144         if (adapter.canAdapt(content)) { // HTT: true branch requires a ReactiveStreamsAdapter on classpath (lives in juneau-rest-server-reactive-reactor, not on this module's test classpath)
145            var adaptation = adapter.adapt(content);
146            if (adaptation.isStream()) // HTT: both branches require a ReactiveStreamsAdapter (see above)
147               return dispatch(opSession, adaptation.stream());
148            res.setContent(adaptation.single());
149            return RESTART;
150         }
151      }
152
153      return NEXT;
154   }
155
156   private int dispatch(RestOpSession opSession, Flow.Publisher<?> pub) throws IOException {
157      var shape = resolveShape(opSession);
158      if (shape == Shape.BUFFER)
159         return handleBuffer(opSession, pub);
160      return handleStream(opSession, pub, shape);
161   }
162
163   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
164   // Buffer shape — collect into a List, hand to the async path.
165   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
166
167   private int handleBuffer(RestOpSession opSession, Flow.Publisher<?> pub) {
168      var cf = new CompletableFuture<List<Object>>();
169      pub.subscribe(new CollectingSubscriber(cf));
170      opSession.getResponse().setContent(cf);
171      return RESTART;
172   }
173
174   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
175   // Streaming shapes — SSE / NDJSON.
176   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
177
178   @SuppressWarnings({
179      "java:S3776", // Streaming setup is branchy by nature (content-type prep + async-vs-sync fallback).
180      "java:S1141"  // Nested try/catch cleanly separates startAsync recovery from the sync fallback.
181   })
182   private int handleStream(RestOpSession opSession, Flow.Publisher<?> pub, Shape shape) throws IOException {
183      var res = opSession.getResponse();
184      prepareStreamingHeaders(res, shape);
185
186      var writer = res.getNegotiatedWriter();
187      FrameEncoder encoder = shape == Shape.SSE
188         ? ReactiveResponseProcessor::writeSseFrame
189         : ReactiveResponseProcessor::writeNdjsonFrame;
190
191      var req = opSession.getRequest().getHttpServletRequest();
192      var mdc = opSession.getRestContext().isMdcAsyncPropagation() // HTT: false branch requires MDC propagation enabled
193         ? MdcAsyncListener.snapshot()
194         : null;
195
196      AsyncContext asyncCtx = null;
197      if (req.isAsyncSupported()) { // HTT: true branch requires a real servlet container (MockServletRequest returns false)
198         try {
199            asyncCtx = req.startAsync();
200         } catch (IllegalStateException e) {
201            asyncCtx = null;  // Already committed, or async not actually supported.
202         }
203      }
204
205      if (asyncCtx != null) { // HTT: true branch requires a real servlet container
206         req.setAttribute(AsyncResponseProcessor.ATTR_ASYNC_DISPATCH_OWNED, Boolean.TRUE);
207         var ac = asyncCtx;
208         ac.setTimeout(0);  // No artificial timeout — streams are paced by the producer / client disconnect.
209         pub.subscribe(new StreamingSubscriber(res, writer, encoder, mdc, t -> completeAsync(ac, res, t)));
210         return FINISHED;
211      }
212
213      // Synchronous fallback (MockServletRequest et al.): block until the publisher terminates.
214      var done = new CompletableFuture<Void>();
215      pub.subscribe(new StreamingSubscriber(res, writer, encoder, mdc,
216         t -> { if (t == null) done.complete(null); else done.completeExceptionally(t); }));
217      awaitSync(done, syncTimeoutMillis(opSession));
218      try {
219         writer.flush();
220         res.flushBuffer();
221      } catch (IOException e) {
222         LOG.log(Level.FINEST, e, () -> "Final flush of synchronous reactive stream failed: " + e.getMessage());
223      }
224      return FINISHED;
225   }
226
227   private static void prepareStreamingHeaders(RestResponse res, Shape shape) {
228      var ct = res.getContentType();
229      if (shape == Shape.SSE) {
230         if (ct == null || ! ct.contains("event-stream"))
231            res.setContentType(SseSerializer.MEDIA_TYPE);
232         res.setHeader("X-Content-Type-Options", "nosniff");
233      } else if (ct == null) {
234         res.setContentType("application/x-ndjson");
235      }
236      res.setHeader("Cache-Control", "no-cache");
237      res.setHeader("Content-Encoding", "identity");
238   }
239
240   @SuppressWarnings({
241      "java:S2142" // Interrupt flag restored before returning.
242   })
243   private static void awaitSync(CompletableFuture<Void> done, long timeoutMs) {
244      try {
245         done.get(timeoutMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);
246      } catch (InterruptedException e) {
247         Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
248      } catch (TimeoutException | ExecutionException e) {
249         LOG.log(Level.FINE, e, () -> "Synchronous reactive stream did not complete cleanly: " + e.getMessage());
250      }
251   }
252
253   private static void completeAsync(AsyncContext asyncCtx, RestResponse res, Throwable error) { // HTT: only reachable from the async path (real servlet container required)
254      try {
255         if (error != null) // HTT: false branch only reachable from async path
256            LOG.log(Level.FINE, error, () -> "Reactive stream terminated with error: " + error.getMessage());
257         if (! res.getHttpServletResponse().isCommitted()) // HTT: true branch (committed=false) only via async path
258            res.flushBuffer();
259      } catch (IOException e) {
260         LOG.log(Level.FINEST, e, () -> "Flush during async stream completion failed: " + e.getMessage());
261      } finally {
262         completeQuietly(asyncCtx);
263      }
264   }
265
266   private static void completeQuietly(AsyncContext asyncCtx) {
267      try {
268         asyncCtx.complete();
269      } catch (IllegalStateException e) {
270         LOG.log(Level.FINEST, e, () -> "AsyncContext.complete() raced with the container: " + e.getMessage());
271      }
272   }
273
274   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
275   // Frame encoders.
276   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
277
278   @SuppressWarnings({
279      "java:S2095" // Writer is response-owned; we never close it.
280   })
281   private static void writeSseFrame(FinishablePrintWriter w, Object element) throws IOException, SerializeException {
282      if (element == null)
283         return;
284      if (element instanceof SseEvent ev) {
285         SseSerializer.DEFAULT.serialize(ev, w);
286         return;
287      }
288      var data = element instanceof CharSequence c ? c.toString() : Json.DEFAULT.of(element);
289      SseSerializer.DEFAULT.serialize(new SseEvent(null, data), w);
290   }
291
292   @SuppressWarnings({
293      "java:S2095" // Writer is response-owned; we never close it.
294   })
295   private static void writeNdjsonFrame(FinishablePrintWriter w, Object element) throws SerializeException {
296      if (element == null)
297         return;
298      var json = element instanceof CharSequence c ? c.toString() : Json.DEFAULT.of(element);
299      w.write(json);
300      w.write("\n");
301      w.flush();
302   }
303
304   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
305   // Shape / timeout resolution.
306   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
307
308   private static Shape resolveShape(RestOpSession opSession) {
309      var res = opSession.getResponse();
310      var ct = res.getContentType();
311      var accept = opSession.getRequest().getHttpServletRequest().getHeader("Accept");
312      var probe = ((ct == null ? "" : ct) + "," + (accept == null ? "" : accept)).toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT);
313      if (probe.contains("event-stream"))
314         return Shape.SSE;
315      if (probe.contains("ndjson") || probe.contains("jsonl") || probe.contains("json5l") || probe.contains("json-seq"))
316         return Shape.NDJSON;
317      return Shape.BUFFER;
318   }
319
320   private static long syncTimeoutMillis(RestOpSession opSession) {
321      var t = opSession.getContext().getAsyncTimeoutMillis();
322      if (t <= 0)
323         t = opSession.getRestContext().getAsyncTimeoutMillis();
324      return t > 0 ? t : AsyncResponseProcessor.DEFAULT_ASYNC_TIMEOUT_MILLIS;
325   }
326
327   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
328   // Adapter discovery (ServiceLoader, cached, defensive against absent backing libraries).
329   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
330
331   private static List<ReactiveStreamsAdapter> adapters() {
332      var a = adapters;
333      if (a == null) {
334         synchronized (ReactiveResponseProcessor.class) {
335            a = adapters;
336            if (a == null) { // HTT: inner DCL null-check missed branch requires concurrent first-load race
337               a = loadAdapters();
338               adapters = a;
339            }
340         }
341      }
342      return a;
343   }
344
345   private static List<ReactiveStreamsAdapter> loadAdapters() {
346      var out = new ArrayList<ReactiveStreamsAdapter>();
347      try {
348         var it = ServiceLoader.load(ReactiveStreamsAdapter.class).iterator();
349         while (it.hasNext())
350            addNextAdapter(it, out);
351      } catch (ServiceConfigurationError | RuntimeException e) {
352         LOG.log(Level.FINE, e, () -> "ServiceLoader for ReactiveStreamsAdapter failed: " + e.getMessage());
353      }
354      return List.copyOf(out);
355   }
356
357   private static void addNextAdapter(Iterator<ReactiveStreamsAdapter> it, List<ReactiveStreamsAdapter> out) {
358      try {
359         out.add(it.next());
360      } catch (ServiceConfigurationError | RuntimeException e) { // HTT: catch branch requires a broken ServiceLoader registration (backing library absent at runtime)
361         LOG.log(Level.FINE, e, () -> "Skipping reactive adapter (backing library likely absent): " + e.getMessage());
362      }
363   }
364
365   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
366   // Subscribers.
367   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
368
369   /** Collects all emitted elements into a list and completes the supplied future (buffer shape). */
370   private static final class CollectingSubscriber implements Flow.Subscriber<Object> {
371      private final CompletableFuture<List<Object>> cf;
372      private final List<Object> items = new ArrayList<>();
373
374      CollectingSubscriber(CompletableFuture<List<Object>> cf) {
375         this.cf = cf;
376      }
377
378      @Override public void onSubscribe(Flow.Subscription s) { s.request(Long.MAX_VALUE); }
379      @Override public void onNext(Object item) { items.add(item); }
380      @Override public void onError(Throwable t) { cf.completeExceptionally(t); }
381      @Override public void onComplete() { cf.complete(items); }
382   }
383
384   /** Writes each emitted element as a wire frame (SSE / NDJSON), one element at a time (bounded backpressure). */
385   private static final class StreamingSubscriber implements Flow.Subscriber<Object> {
386      private final RestResponse res;
387      private final FinishablePrintWriter writer;
388      private final FrameEncoder encoder;
389      private final Map<String,String> mdc;
390      private final Consumer<Throwable> onTerminate;
391      private final AtomicBoolean terminated = new AtomicBoolean();
392      private volatile boolean wrote;
393      private Flow.Subscription subscription;
394
395      StreamingSubscriber(RestResponse res, FinishablePrintWriter writer, FrameEncoder encoder,
396            Map<String,String> mdc, Consumer<Throwable> onTerminate) {
397         this.res = res;
398         this.writer = writer;
399         this.encoder = encoder;
400         this.mdc = mdc;
401         this.onTerminate = onTerminate;
402      }
403
404      @Override public void onSubscribe(Flow.Subscription s) {
405         subscription = s;
406         s.request(1);
407      }
408
409      @Override public void onNext(Object item) {
410         try {
411            withMdc(() -> writeFrame(item));
412            subscription.request(1);
413         } catch (RuntimeException e) {
414            subscription.cancel();
415            terminate(unwrap(e));
416         }
417      }
418
419      @Override public void onError(Throwable t) { terminate(t); }
420      @Override public void onComplete() { terminate(null); }
421
422      private void writeFrame(Object item) {
423         try {
424            encoder.write(writer, item);
425            writer.flush();
426            res.flushBuffer();
427            wrote = true;
428         } catch (IOException | SerializeException e) {
429            throw new IllegalStateException(e);
430         }
431      }
432
433      private void withMdc(Runnable r) {
434         MdcAsyncListener.wrap((BiConsumer<Object,Throwable>) (v, e) -> r.run(), mdc).accept(null, null);
435      }
436
437      private void terminate(Throwable t) {
438         if (! terminated.compareAndSet(false, true)) // HTT: false branch requires concurrent double-terminate
439            return;
440         withMdc(() -> {
441            if (t != null && ! wrote) {
442               try {
443                  if (! res.getHttpServletResponse().isCommitted()) // HTT: true branch (isCommitted=false) only via async path
444                     res.getHttpServletResponse().sendError(SC_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);
445               } catch (IOException e) {
446                  LOG.log(Level.FINEST, e, () -> "sendError on pre-write stream failure failed: " + e.getMessage());
447               }
448            }
449         });
450         onTerminate.accept(t);
451      }
452
453      private static Throwable unwrap(Throwable t) { // HTT: only called when writeFrame throws RuntimeException (serializer failure)
454         if ((t instanceof IllegalStateException || t instanceof CompletionException) && t.getCause() != null)
455            return t.getCause();
456         return t;
457      }
458   }
459}
